reply_text:
​@GTARanger1992you have a good ear!

This is that exact version, sung by Ralph Stanley specifically for O Brother. The Coens originally asked for the song to have musical accompaniment for the scene, but Stanley (who was of course very well-versed in traditional Appalachian music) recommended they record it acapella in the traditional ballad style instead. And he was SO right. Such a powerful song.

As a fan of traditional music myself, I'm thrilled to see his rendition appear in popular culture again. Ballad singing is an underappreciated chapter in the massive and wonderful tome of American folk, and it's always a delight when it gets some attention.

If you like this, you might get a kick out of Berzilla Wallen's version, entitled "Conversation with Death." Prime little old lady goth. You can tell she's really reveling the creepy imagery as she sings it, hehe.
